title: Floral Crispy Egyptian Rice Bowl
description: A fragrant Egyptian rice bowl bursting with citrus, toasted quinoa, and pecorino, finished with a crunchy pistachio and marigold garnish.

ingredients.name: saffron threads
ingredients.amount: 1 pinch, steeped in 1 tbsp hot water

ingredients.name: pistachios & marigold petals
ingredients.amount: 2 tbsp pistachios & 1 tbsp marigold petals, chopped

instructions: Rinse the basmati rice under cold water until the water runs clear. Drain well.
In a medium saucepan, bring the water to a boil. Add the saffron steeped in 1 tbsp hot water, lemon zest, lemon juice, garlic-infused oil, cumin, coriander, and black pepper; stir to combine.

Add the rice, reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed. Remove from heat and fluff with a fork; keep covered while you finish the remaining steps.
While the rice cooks, toast the quinoa in a dry skillet over medium heat until lightly golden and fragrant, about 5 minutes. Transfer to a bowl and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the pistachios and marigold petals, and toast for 2 minutes until the pistachios are crisp and the petals are fragrant. Toss them into the quinoa and mix.
When the rice is ready, transfer it to a large serving bowl and spread the toasted quinoa-pistachio mixture over the rice.
Sprinkle the pecorino shavings evenly over the top.
Optionally garnish with a few extra marigold petals and a lighter drizzle of garlic-infused oil for aroma.
Serve immediately, letting guests scoop the crispy rice and cheese into their bowls.
